org.arakhne.neteditor.figlayout.force

Class ForceBasedFigureLayout

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.figlayout.AbstractFigureLayout
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.figlayout.force.ForceBasedFigureLayout
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ViewComponentConstants, FigureLayout, FigureLayoutConstants, ForceBasedConstants

    public class ForceBasedFigureLayout
extends AbstractFigureLayout
implements ForceBasedConstants
    This class provides the implementation of a force-based laying-out algorithm.

    The force toward and away from nodes is calculated according to Hooke's Law and Coulomb's Law.

    Since:
    16.0
    Version:
    NetEditor Figure Layout $Revision$ 2013-10-23 21:03:08
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    See Also:
    "http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Force-based_algorithms_(graph_drawing)"
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-figlayout

      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      float getCoulombConstant()
      Replies the Coulomb's constant to use.
      float getDamping()
      Replies the damping constant.
      float getMaximalKinematicEnergy()
      Replies the maximal kinematic energy up to the algorithm is able to reach.
      FigureMassCalculator getNodeMassCalculator()
      Replies the calculator of the node's masses.
      float getPreferredInterNodeSpace()
      Replies the preferred space between two nodes (insets are outside this preferred space).
      float getRandomCoordinateSpaceSize()
      Replies the size of the coordinate space that is used to compute a coordinate randomly.
      float getSpringConstant()
      Replies the spring constant to use.
      float getTimeStep()
      Replies the time step used by the algorithm.
      Undoable layoutFigures(Collection<? extends Figure> figures)
      Run the laying-out algorithm for the given figures.

      Set the calculator of the node's masses.
      void setPreferredInterNodeSpace(float space)
      Set the preferred space between two nodes (insets are outside this preferred space).
      void setRandomCoordinateSpaceSize(float size)
      Set the size of the coordinate space that is used to compute a coordinate randomly.
      void setSpringConstant(float k)
      Set the spring constant to use.
      void setTimeStep(float step)
      Set the time step used by the algorithm.

    • Method Detail

      • setPreferredInterNodeSpace

        public void setPreferredInterNodeSpace(float space)
        Set the preferred space between two nodes (insets are outside this preferred space).
        Parameters:
        space -

      • getPreferredInterNodeSpace

        public float getPreferredInterNodeSpace()
        Replies the preferred space between two nodes (insets are outside this preferred space).
        Returns:
        the preferred space.

      • setNodeMassCalculator

        public void setNodeMassCalculator(FigureMassCalculator calculator)
        Set the calculator of the node's masses.
        Parameters:
        calculator -

      • getNodeMassCalculator

        public FigureMassCalculator getNodeMassCalculator()
        Replies the calculator of the node's masses.
        Returns:
        the calculator of the masses.

      • getRandomCoordinateSpaceSize

        public float getRandomCoordinateSpaceSize()
        Replies the size of the coordinate space that is used to compute a coordinate randomly.
        Returns:
        the size of the random coordinate space.

      • setRandomCoordinateSpaceSize

        public void setRandomCoordinateSpaceSize(float size)
        Set the size of the coordinate space that is used to compute a coordinate randomly.
        Parameters:
        size - is the size of the random coordinate space; stricly positive.

      • getMaximalKinematicEnergy

        public float getMaximalKinematicEnergy()
        Replies the maximal kinematic energy up to the algorithm is able to reach.
        Returns:
        the maximal kinematic energy, stricly positive.

      • setMaximalKinematicEnergy

        public void setMaximalKinematicEnergy(float energy)
        Set the maximal kinematic energy up to the algorithm is able to reach.
        Parameters:
        energy - is the maximal kinematic energy, stricly positive.

      • getCoulombConstant

        public float getCoulombConstant()
        Replies the Coulomb's constant to use. Default is ForceBasedConstants.DEFAULT_COULOMB_CONSTANT.
        Returns:
        the Coulomb's constant, stricly positive.

      • setCoulombConstant

        public void setCoulombConstant(float k)
        Set the Coulomb's constant to use.
        Parameters:
        k - the Coulomb's constant, stricly positive.

      • getSpringConstant

        public float getSpringConstant()
        Replies the spring constant to use. Default is ForceBasedConstants.DEFAULT_SPRING_CONSTANT.
        Returns:
        the spring constant, stricly positive.

      • setSpringConstant

        public void setSpringConstant(float k)
        Set the spring constant to use.
        Parameters:
        k - the spring constant, stricly positive.

      • getDamping

        public float getDamping()
        Replies the damping constant. The damping constant is in (0;1) exclusive.
        Returns:
        the damping constant.

      • setDamping

        public void setDamping(float damping)
        Set the damping constant. The damping constant is in (0;1) exclusive.
        Parameters:
        damping - is the damping constant.

      • getTimeStep

        public float getTimeStep()
        Replies the time step used by the algorithm. The time step is the amount of time during which a node is atomically moved.
        Returns:
        the time step, stricly positive.

      • setTimeStep

        public void setTimeStep(float step)
        Set the time step used by the algorithm. The time step is the amount of time during which a node is atomically moved.
        Parameters:
        step - is the time step, stricly positive.

      • layoutFigures

        public Undoable layoutFigures(Collection<? extends Figure> figures)
        Run the laying-out algorithm for the given figures.
        Specified by:
        layoutFigures in interface FigureLayout
        Parameters:
        figures - are the figures to laying out.
        Returns:
        the undoable edit that permits to revert the laying out; or null if the layout does not supports the undo/redo feature or it has nothing to undo.
